vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely unsupported by visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is not visible in the yellow pot. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as living specimens. P3 (Pothos) is absent; only the white rabbit anchor remains in a black pot.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss (Yellow pot appears empty/soil only)
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss (Pothos foliage absent; only anchor present)
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No identifiable silver guest foliage)

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty soil surface, no vegetative growth detected.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Black_Pot: Contains white rabbit anchor; no biological plant matter visible.
plant_audit.P3_Black_Pot: See P2/P4 entry; no Pothos detected.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dark and potentially desiccated or saturated; lighting makes texture analysis difficult.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of a spoon in the yellow pot (T-3) and a cylindrical object (T-2) suggests human intervention/maintenance tools left in the biome.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_T-4: Initial state shows empty pots with anchor.
temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-2: Introduction of foreign objects (spoon, cylinder) into the biome.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_current: Removal of foreign objects; return to a state of total vegetative absence.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of total biological failure. No active photosynthesis or plant structures are present. The environment is essentially a collection of empty containers.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a complete collapse of the botanical registry. Across the five-day observation period, the pots have remained devoid of the expected plant life. The only consistent element is the white rabbit anchor. The presence of household items like a spoon suggests the area is being used for non-botanical purposes or that the plants were removed prior to the start of this sequence.
